notes: goldEvidence is intentionally empty in this version. Follow-up curation is required to add frozen-corpus passage anchors before open-book retrieval grading can use this dataset for evidence matching.

questions.title: Tab Definition
questions.question: I'm building a tab bar and want to give each tab an icon and title. What's the modern way to define tabs in a TabView?
questions.pitfall: Using the deprecated `tabItem()` modifier.

goldEvidence.quote: To build tabs with the modern API, use Tab in the content builder of the TabView.
A tab in a tab view.

questions.title: Programmatic Tab Selection
questions.question: I need to programmatically switch tabs in my app. I'm thinking of using integers to track which tab is selected — is that the standard approach?
questions.pitfall: Using `Int` or `String` for tab selection, which is fragile and unclear.

goldEvidence.quote: You can perform programmatic selection by passing a binding to the selection parameter.

questions.title: Hierarchical Navigation Setup
questions.question: I'm setting up hierarchical navigation where tapping a list row pushes a detail screen. What's the recommended container and link pattern?
questions.pitfall: Using `NavigationView` with `NavigationLink(destination:)`.

questions.title: Toolbar Button in Top-Right Corner
questions.question: I want to place an Edit button in the top-right corner of my navigation bar. What placement value should I use in a ToolbarItem?
questions.pitfall: Using the deprecated `.navigationBarLeading` and `.navigationBarTrailing` placements.

questions.title: Styling Text with a Custom Color
questions.question: I'm styling text with a custom color from my asset catalog. There seem to be multiple options for setting text color in SwiftUI — which modifier should I use?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `foregroundStyle()`, which works with any `ShapeStyle` including colors, gradients, and hierarchical styles:

```swift
Text("Hello")
    .foregroundStyle(.red)

Text("Custom")
    .foregroundStyle(Color("BrandBlue"))
```
questions.pitfall: Using the deprecated `foregroundColor()` modifier.

questions.title: Differently-Styled Text on One Line
questions.question: I need to display "Hello" in red and "World" in blue on the same line as a single text element. How do I compose differently-styled text fragments?
questions.pitfall: Using `Text` concatenation with `+`, which is deprecated.

questions.title: Rounding Corners on a Container
questions.question: I need to round the corners of a VStack that has a background. What's the correct way to clip a view to rounded corners?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `clipShape()` with a rounded rectangle:

```swift
VStack { /* content */ }
    .clipShape(.rect(cornerRadius: 12))
```
questions.pitfall: Using the deprecated `cornerRadius()` modifier.

questions.title: Adding a Border to a Filled Shape
questions.question: I want to add a stroke border around a `RoundedRectangle` — should I use an `.overlay` with a stroked shape on top?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Since iOS 17, you can chain `.fill()` and `.stroke()` directly on a shape:

```swift
RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 12)
    .fill(.blue)
    .stroke(.white, lineWidth: 2)
```
questions.pitfall: Using an `.overlay` with a separate stroked shape, which was required before iOS 17.

questions.title: Smooth Apple-Style Rounded Corners
questions.question: I want smooth, Apple-style rounded corners on my card shapes. Do I need to pass a corner style parameter to get the continuous rounding effect?
questions.referenceAnswer:
No. The default corner style for `RoundedRectangle` is already `.continuous`:

```swift
RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 12) // .continuous is the default
```
questions.pitfall: Explicitly passing `style: .continuous` when it's already the default.

questions.title: Shared Data Model for Multiple Views
questions.question: I'm building a shopping cart that multiple views need to read and write to. What's the modern way to create a shared data model in SwiftUI?
questions.pitfall: Using `ObservableObject`, `@Published`, `@StateObject`, `@ObservedObject`, or `@EnvironmentObject`.

questions.title: Thread Safety for @Observable Classes
questions.question: I have an `@Observable` class — do I need to annotate it with `@MainActor`?
questions.pitfall: Omitting `@MainActor`, which can lead to data races and concurrency warnings.

questions.title: Persisting UserDefaults in an Observable Class
questions.question: Can I put an `@AppStorage` property inside my `@Observable` class so the view updates when UserDefaults changes?
questions.referenceAnswer: No. `@AppStorage` inside an `@Observable` class will not trigger view updates. `@AppStorage` is designed for views, not classes. Read from `UserDefaults` directly in the class and use `@AppStorage` in the view layer.
questions.pitfall: Placing `@AppStorage` inside an `@Observable` class and expecting it to drive view refreshes.

questions.title: Saving on Every Keystroke
questions.question: Can I create a custom binding inline so I can intercept and save on every keystroke?
questions.pitfall: Creating `Binding(get: { model.username }, set: { model.username = $0; model.save() })` inline in the view body. *(Source: gpt54minihigh phrasing)*

questions.title: Numeric Input Field
questions.question: I need a `TextField` for the user to enter their age as a number. Should I bind it to a `String` and convert with `Int()` afterward?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use the numeric `TextField` initializer with a `format` parameter:

```swift
@State private var age = 0

TextField("Enter your age", value: $age, format: .number)
    .keyboardType(.numberPad)
```
questions.pitfall: Binding to a `String`, manually converting, and losing type safety.

questions.title: User-Facing Search Text Matching
questions.question: I'm filtering an array of names using `name.contains(searchText)`. Is this the right approach for user-facing search?
questions.referenceAnswer:
No. Use `localizedStandardContains()` for user-facing search — it handles case, diacritics, and locale correctly:

```swift
let results = names.filter { $0.localizedStandardContains(searchText) }
```

This lets "cafe" match "Cafe" and "Caf\u00e9".
questions.pitfall: Using `contains()` which is case-sensitive and doesn't handle diacritics.

questions.title: Empty Search State
questions.question: When the search returns no results, I want to show a "No results found" message with the user's query. What's the best way to build that?
questions.pitfall: Building a custom empty-state view or passing the search text explicitly.

questions.title: Displaying Prices and Currency
questions.question: I need to display a price with proper currency formatting and two decimal places. What's the best approach in SwiftUI?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use SwiftUI's built-in format styles:

```swift
Text(price, format: .currency(code: "USD"))

// Or for a plain number:
Text(price, format: .number.precision(.fractionLength(2)))
```

These handle localization (decimal separators, currency symbols) automatically.
questions.pitfall: Using `String(format: "%.2f", price)` which doesn't localize.

questions.title: Formatting Dates for Display
questions.question: I need to show dates in my app in a user-friendly format. Should I create a date formatter property and store it on my view?
questions.referenceAnswer:
No. Use `Text` with a `format` parameter directly:

```swift
Text(event.date, format: .dateTime.day().month().year())
```

This is cleaner, more performant, and automatically localizes.
questions.pitfall: Creating and managing `DateFormatter` instances when SwiftUI's format-style `Text` initializer handles it natively.

questions.title: Locale-Safe Date Display
questions.question: I want to display a date in the user's local format. Is it safe to hardcode a date format string like "MM/dd/yyyy"?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Prefer `FormatStyle` APIs:

```swift
Text(date, format: .dateTime.month(.twoDigits).day(.twoDigits).year())
```

Manual format strings with `"yyyy"` can produce wrong years in some calendar localizations. `FormatStyle` handles localization automatically.
questions.pitfall: Using manual date format strings that don't adapt to user locale.

questions.title: Reacting to State Changes
questions.question: I need to run code whenever a search field's value changes. How do I observe state changes in SwiftUI, and what parameters does the change callback receive?
questions.pitfall: Using `.onChange(of: value) { newValue in ... }` — the 1-parameter variant is deprecated.

questions.title: Animating a Specific Value Change
questions.question: I want to animate whenever `score` changes — can I just add `.animation(.bouncy)` to my view without specifying a value?
questions.referenceAnswer:
No. Always specify which value to watch:

```swift
Text("\(score)")
    .animation(.bouncy, value: score)
```

The value-less variant is deprecated and causes unpredictable animations on unrelated state changes.
questions.pitfall: Using `.animation(.bouncy)` without `value:`.

questions.title: Chaining Sequential Animations
questions.question: I want to chain two animations — scale up, then scale back down. Should I use a delayed dispatch to time the second animation after the first finishes?
questions.pitfall: Using `DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter` with a guessed delay, which doesn't align with actual animation duration.

questions.title: Making a Custom Shape Animatable
questions.question: I'm creating a custom shape that animates between states. What's the modern way to make a shape's properties animate smoothly?
questions.pitfall: Manually implementing the `Animatable` protocol and writing `var animatableData`.

questions.title: Sizing a View to Half the Available Width
questions.question: I need to size a view to exactly half the available width. How do I measure the container's dimensions in SwiftUI?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `containerRelativeFrame()`:

```swift
Image("hero")
    .containerRelativeFrame(.horizontal) { width, _ in
        width / 2
    }
```

For complex needs, consider `visualEffect()`. Only use `GeometryReader` as a last resort.
questions.pitfall: Using `UIScreen.main.bounds` which is deprecated, doesn't account for multitasking, and doesn't adapt to container changes.

questions.title: Reading a Parent View's Size
questions.question: I need to position child elements based on how much space the parent has. What are my options for reading container dimensions?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Modern alternatives (prefer in order):

1. `containerRelativeFrame()` — size relative to container
2. `visualEffect()` — apply effects based on geometry without affecting layout
3. `Layout` protocol — custom layout logic
4. `GeometryReader` — last resort (greedily expands, can cause layout issues)
questions.pitfall: Reaching for `GeometryReader` as a first choice.

questions.title: Accessibility of an Icon-Only Button
questions.question: I have a button that only shows an SF Symbol icon. Does this need any special accessibility work?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Yes — include a text label even if only the icon is displayed:

```swift
Button("Delete", systemImage: "trash", action: delete)
```

SwiftUI shows the icon and uses the text for VoiceOver.
questions.pitfall: Creating icon-only buttons without text labels, invisible to VoiceOver and Voice Control.

questions.title: Making an Image Interactive
questions.question: I want users to tap on a photo to open it full-screen. What's the best way to make an image tappable?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use a `Button`, not `onTapGesture`:

```swift
Button("View Photo", systemImage: "photo") {
    showPhoto()
}
```

`onTapGesture` doesn't convey interactivity to assistive technologies. Only use it when you need tap location or count.
questions.pitfall: Using `onTapGesture()` for interactive elements, lacking button semantics.

questions.title: Custom Font Size That Respects User Preferences
questions.question: I need text at a specific point size that's larger than body. How do I set a custom font size that still respects the user's text size preferences?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `@ScaledMetric` (iOS 18 and earlier) or `.scaled(by:)` (iOS 26+):

```swift
@ScaledMetric private var iconSize = 24.0

// iOS 26+
.font(.body.scaled(by: 1.5))
```

Prefer built-in Dynamic Type sizes (`.title`, `.headline`, `.body`) when possible.
questions.pitfall: Using `.font(.system(size: 24))` which ignores Dynamic Type.

questions.title: Slider Layout in a Form
questions.question: I have a Slider inside a Form with a Text label in an HStack. How do I get the label and slider to lay out correctly?
questions.pitfall: Using `HStack { Text("Volume"); Slider(...) }` which doesn't follow Form layout conventions.

questions.title: Multiline Text Input with Placeholder
questions.question: I need a multiline text input with placeholder text for user notes. What's the simplest SwiftUI approach?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `TextField` with `axis: .vertical`:

```swift
TextField("Enter your notes...", text: $notes, axis: .vertical)
    .lineLimit(5...)
```

`TextEditor` doesn't support placeholder text. Only use it for full-screen editing.
questions.pitfall: Using `TextEditor` which lacks placeholder support.

questions.title: Presenting a Detail Sheet from an Optional
questions.question: I have an optional selected item that should open a detail sheet when non-nil. What's the cleanest way to present it?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `sheet(item:)`:

```swift
.sheet(item: $selectedItem) { item in
    DetailView(item: item)
}
```
questions.pitfall: Using `sheet(isPresented:)` with manual `if let` unwrapping inside.

questions.title: Where to Attach a Confirmation Dialog
questions.question: Where should I attach my `confirmationDialog()` modifier — on the root NavigationStack, or on the button that triggers it?
questions.pitfall: Attaching to a parent container, which breaks the source animation.

questions.title: Slow-Loading ScrollView with Many Items
questions.question: I have 500 items in a ScrollView with a VStack. The initial load is slow — what's going wrong?
questions.pitfall: Using `VStack` inside `ScrollView` for large data sets.

questions.title: Controlling Scroll Indicator Visibility
questions.question: I want to hide scroll indicators in my ScrollView. What's the modern modifier for controlling indicator visibility?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `.scrollIndicators(.hidden)`:

```swift
ScrollView { /* content */ }
    .scrollIndicators(.hidden)
```
questions.pitfall: Using the old `ScrollView(showsIndicators: false)` initializer parameter.

questions.title: Optimizing Scroll-Edge Rendering
questions.question: My ScrollView has a solid, opaque background. Is there anything I can do to optimize its scroll-edge rendering?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `scrollContentBackground(.visible)`:

```swift
ScrollView { /* content */ }
    .scrollContentBackground(.visible)
```

This tells SwiftUI the background is opaque so it can optimize edge effects.
questions.pitfall: Not applying this when the background is known to be solid.

questions.title: Loading Data When a View Appears
questions.question: I need to fetch data from an API when a view first appears. What's the recommended way to kick off async work tied to a view's lifecycle?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `.task()`:

```swift
.task {
    await loadData()
}
```

It supports `async`/`await` natively and automatically cancels when the view disappears.
questions.pitfall: Using `onAppear` for async work, requiring manual `Task` creation and lacking auto-cancellation.

questions.title: Expensive Work in a View Initializer
questions.question: My view initializer fetches data from Core Data and sorts it. Is there a better place to put this work?
questions.pitfall: Performing fetching, sorting, or other non-trivial work in `init()`.

questions.title: Toggling Modifiers with if/else
questions.question: I want to change a view's opacity based on a boolean. Should I use `if isActive { view.opacity(1) } else { view.opacity(0.5) }` in my body?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use a ternary expression:

```swift
MyView()
    .opacity(isActive ? 1 : 0.5)
```

if/else creates `_ConditionalContent`, destroying structural identity — SwiftUI treats the branches as different views, losing state and animations.
questions.pitfall: Using if/else branching to toggle modifier values.

questions.title: Returning Different View Types from a Function
questions.question: I have a function that returns different view types depending on a condition, and I'm getting type errors. How do I handle heterogeneous return types from a view-building function?
questions.pitfall: Wrapping views in `AnyView`, which erases type information and hurts SwiftUI's diffing performance.

questions.title: Breaking Up a Long View Body
questions.question: My view body is very long. Should I extract parts into computed properties that return `some View`, decorated with `@ViewBuilder`?
questions.pitfall: Extracting into `@ViewBuilder` computed properties, which loses performance benefits.

questions.title: Multiple View Types in One File
questions.question: I have my ContentView, HeaderView, and FooterView all in the same Swift file. Is that OK?
questions.referenceAnswer: Each type should be in its own file (`ContentView.swift`, `HeaderView.swift`, `FooterView.swift`). This improves discoverability, reduces merge conflicts, and follows Swift project conventions.
questions.pitfall: Placing multiple type definitions in a single file.

questions.title: Defining a Custom Environment Value
questions.question: I want to pass a custom theme value through the SwiftUI environment to all child views. What's the modern way to define a custom environment value?
questions.pitfall: Manually creating an `EnvironmentKey` struct with `defaultValue` and extending `EnvironmentValues`.

questions.title: Triggering Haptic Feedback
questions.question: I want to trigger haptic feedback when a user completes an action. What's the SwiftUI-native way to do this without bridging to UIKit?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `sensoryFeedback()`:

```swift
Button("Complete", action: markComplete)
    .sensoryFeedback(.success, trigger: isCompleted)
```

Available types: `.success`, `.warning`, `.error`, `.impact`, `.selection`.
questions.pitfall: Using UIKit's `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator` or `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator`.

questions.title: Updating the UI After a Background Task
questions.question: After a background network call completes, I need to update the UI. How do I ensure code runs on the main thread in modern Swift?
questions.pitfall: Using `DispatchQueue.main.async` or `DispatchQueue.global().async` instead of Swift concurrency.

questions.title: Adding a Delay in an Async Context
questions.question: I need to add a 2-second delay before retrying a failed network request. How do I pause execution in an async function?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `Task.sleep(for:)` with a `Duration`:

```swift
try await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(2))
```
questions.pitfall: Using `Task.sleep(nanoseconds:)` which is hard to read and error-prone.

questions.title: Thread-Safe Shared Cache
questions.question: I have a shared mutable cache that multiple concurrent tasks read from and write to. How do I make this thread-safe in modern Swift?
questions.pitfall: Using `DispatchQueue` with sync/async for manual synchronization.

questions.title: Getting the User's Documents Directory
questions.question: I need to save a file to the user's documents directory. What's the simplest way to get the documents URL in modern Swift?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use the `URL` static property:

```swift
let documentsURL = URL.documentsDirectory
let fileURL = URL.documentsDirectory.appending(path: "data.json")
```
questions.pitfall: Using the verbose `FileManager.default.urls(for: .documentDirectory, in: .userDomainMask).first!`.

questions.title: Replacing Substrings in a String
questions.question: I need to replace all occurrences of a substring. What's the preferred Swift-native string method?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `replacing()`:

```swift
let result = myString.replacing("a", with: "b")
```

Prefer Swift-native string methods over Foundation equivalents.
questions.pitfall: Using the Foundation method `replacingOccurrences(of:with:)`.

questions.title: ForEach with Index and Item
questions.question: I want to iterate over an array with indices using `ForEach`. Should I convert `items.enumerated()` to an `Array` first, like `Array(items.enumerated())`?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Don't convert to an array — use `enumerated()` directly, and identify by the element, not the offset:

```swift
ForEach(items.enumerated(), id: \.element.id) { index, item in
    Text("\(index + 1). \(item.name)")
}
```
questions.pitfall: Wrapping in `Array()` unnecessarily and using `\.offset` for identity, which is fragile when the array changes.

questions.title: Avoiding Repeated id: Parameters
questions.question: My data types don't have a dedicated ID property. I've been specifying `id: \.name` at every ForEach and List call site. Is there a better pattern?
questions.pitfall: Repeating `id: \.someProperty` at every call site instead of conforming once.

questions.title: Displaying a Web Page in SwiftUI
questions.question: I need to display a web page inside my SwiftUI app. What's the simplest approach on iOS 26?
questions.pitfall: Creating a `UIViewRepresentable` wrapper around `WKWebView` when a native view exists.

questions.title: Storing Sensitive Data Across Launches
questions.question: Can I store the user's authentication token in `@AppStorage` so it persists across launches?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Never store secrets in `@AppStorage` — it uses `UserDefaults`, which is unencrypted. Use the Keychain:

```swift
let query: [String: Any] = [
    kSecClass as String: kSecClassGenericPassword,
    kSecAttrAccount as String: "authToken",
    kSecValueData as String: tokenData
]
SecItemAdd(query as CFDictionary, nil)
```

`@AppStorage` is fine for non-sensitive preferences (theme, font size).
questions.pitfall: Storing secrets in unencrypted `UserDefaults`.

questions.title: Displaying an Icon Next to a Label
questions.question: Should I use an HStack with an Image and Text to show an icon next to a label?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `Label`:

```swift
Label("Downloads", systemImage: "arrow.down.circle")
```

`Label` handles layout across contexts (list rows, menus, buttons) and provides proper accessibility semantics.
questions.pitfall: Using `HStack { Image(systemName:); Text() }` which doesn't adapt to context.

questions.title: Large Animations and User Sensitivity
questions.question: I have a large slide-in animation when my view appears. Do I need to check any user settings before playing it?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Check `accessibilityReduceMotion` and replace large motion with opacity fades:

```swift
@Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) private var reduceMotion

ContentView()
    .transition(reduceMotion ? .opacity : .slide)
```
questions.pitfall: Playing large animations without checking user preferences, causing discomfort for motion-sensitive users.

questions.title: Counting Items That Match a Condition
questions.question: I need to count how many items in an array match a condition. Is filtering then counting the best approach?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use `count(where:)`:

```swift
let completedCount = tasks.count(where: \.isCompleted)
```
questions.pitfall: Using `filter { }.count` which allocates an intermediate array.

questions.title: Capturing a SwiftUI View as a Bitmap
questions.question: I need to capture a SwiftUI view as a bitmap image for sharing. What's the SwiftUI-native approach?
questions.pitfall: Using UIKit's `UIGraphicsImageRenderer`, which requires bridging through a hosting controller.

questions.title: Unique Constraints in SwiftData with CloudKit
questions.question: I want to enforce uniqueness on my SwiftData model's `email` property. Should I add `@Attribute(.unique)` to it?
questions.referenceAnswer: If using CloudKit sync, never use `@Attribute(.unique)` — CloudKit doesn't support unique constraints and sync will fail. Handle uniqueness in application logic or check before insert.
questions.pitfall: Using `@Attribute(.unique)` in a CloudKit-synced model.

questions.title: SwiftData Relationships with CloudKit
questions.question: My SwiftData model has a `tags` relationship defined as `var tags: [Tag]`. Does it need to be optional for CloudKit?
questions.pitfall: Non-optional relationships causing CloudKit sync failures.

questions.title: Displaying a Person's Full Name
questions.question: I have `firstName` and `lastName` strings. Should I display them as `Text("\(firstName) \(lastName)")`?
questions.pitfall: String interpolation assumes Western name ordering, breaking for other cultures.

questions.title: Overlaying a Badge on an Image
questions.question: I want to overlay a notification badge on the top-right corner of a product image. What's the correct overlay syntax in modern SwiftUI?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use the trailing-closure form:

```swift
Image("product")
    .overlay(alignment: .topTrailing) {
        BadgeView()
    }
```
questions.pitfall: Using the deprecated `.overlay(SomeView(), alignment:)` syntax where the view is the first argument.

questions.title: Sharing Mutable State with a Child View
questions.question: I declared `@State var count = 0` in my view. A child view also needs to mutate it — should the child also declare `@State var count`?
questions.pitfall: Declaring `@State` in multiple views for the same data, creating independent copies.

questions.title: Setting Up SwiftUI Previews
questions.question: I need to set up previews for my view. What's the modern preview syntax?
questions.pitfall: Using the legacy `PreviewProvider` protocol with `static var previews`.

questions.title: Menu with Only an Icon Label
questions.question: I'm creating a `Menu` with just an SF Symbol icon as its label. Is that sufficient?
questions.referenceAnswer:
No — include a text label:

```swift
Menu("Options", systemImage: "ellipsis.circle") {
    Button("Edit", action: edit)
    Button("Delete", role: .destructive, action: delete)
}
```

The text label is used by VoiceOver even if only the icon is displayed.
questions.pitfall: Icon-only Menu labels, invisible to assistive technology.

questions.title: Color-Only Status Indicators
questions.question: I'm using colored circles to indicate status: red for error, yellow for warning, green for OK. Is this enough?
questions.pitfall: Using only color to convey meaning — unusable for colorblind users.

questions.title: Type-Safe Asset Catalog References
questions.question: I'm loading images from my asset catalog using string names. Is there a safer way to reference assets that catches errors at compile time?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use generated asset symbols:

```swift
Image(.avatar)  // instead of Image("avatar")
```

This provides compile-time safety — errors surface if the asset is renamed or deleted.
questions.pitfall: String-based `Image("avatar")` which fails silently at runtime.

questions.title: Accepting Child Content in a Custom View
questions.question: I'm building a `CardView<Content: View>` that accepts child content via a `let content: () -> Content` closure property. Is this the right way?
questions.pitfall: Storing `() -> Content` as an escaping closure, which has overhead and is called repeatedly.

questions.title: Parsing an ISO 8601 Date String
questions.question: I have an ISO 8601 date string from an API that I need to convert to a `Date`. What's the cleanest approach?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use the modern `Date` initializer with a parse strategy:

```swift
let date = try Date("2026-04-07T12:00:00Z", strategy: .iso8601)
```
questions.pitfall: Creating an `ISO8601DateFormatter` instance when a one-line initializer exists.

questions.title: Handling Errors from User Actions
questions.question: In my `catch` block, I'm calling `print(error.localizedDescription)` to log the error. Is there anything else I should do?
questions.pitfall: Using `print()` as the only error handling for user-initiated actions.

questions.title: Missing Combine at Runtime
questions.question: I'm using a Combine publisher for debouncing in my ObservableObject, but I'm getting a runtime error about missing Combine. What's wrong?
questions.pitfall: Assuming `import SwiftUI` provides Combine implicitly.

questions.title: Subtle Text Hierarchy with Transparency
questions.question: I want my subtitle text to be slightly transparent to create visual hierarchy. Should I use `.opacity(0.6)` on the Text?
questions.referenceAnswer:
Use hierarchical styles which adapt to context automatically:

```swift
Text("Subtitle")
    .foregroundStyle(.secondary)
```

Available: `.primary`, `.secondary`, `.tertiary`, `.quaternary`. These adapt to light/dark mode and accessibility settings.
questions.pitfall: Using `.opacity(0.6)` which doesn't adapt to different appearances.

questions.title: Where to Attach an Accessibility Label
questions.question: Can I add a hidden `accessibilityLabel` to the Image inside my button instead of labeling the button itself?
questions.referenceAnswer: No. The accessibility label should be attached to the interactive element — the button — not the decorative image inside it. Use `Button("Delete", systemImage: "trash", action: delete)` which provides the label to the button itself. If you label only the image, VoiceOver may not associate the label with the tappable control.
questions.pitfall: Attaching accessibility labels to decorative child views instead of the interactive parent.

questions.title: Hiding an Interactive Element from VoiceOver
questions.question: Is it okay to use `accessibilityHidden(true)` on an interactive control to stop VoiceOver from reading it?
questions.referenceAnswer: Never hide interactive elements from VoiceOver. This makes the control completely invisible to users who rely on assistive technology. Instead, provide a proper text label. If VoiceOver reads something unexpected, fix the label rather than hiding the element.
questions.pitfall: Using `accessibilityHidden(true)` on buttons or other interactive controls.

questions.title: Mixing Old and New Navigation APIs
questions.question: Can I nest a `NavigationStack` inside a `NavigationView` to get the best of both APIs during a migration?
questions.referenceAnswer: No. Replace `NavigationView` entirely with `NavigationStack`. Nesting them causes undefined behavior, double navigation bars, and broken push/pop animations. `NavigationStack` is the complete replacement, not a supplement.
questions.pitfall: Wrapping `NavigationStack` inside `NavigationView` during an incremental migration.

questions.title: Heavy Computation in a View Body
questions.question: Is it okay to do expensive work like JSON parsing in the view body, since SwiftUI caches view values between renders?
questions.pitfall: Assuming SwiftUI caches body computations. It doesn't — body runs on every state change.

questions.title: Calling Async Code from a Button
questions.question: Can I call an async function directly inside a button's action closure?
questions.pitfall: Attempting to use `await` in a synchronous button closure.
